endangeredFish.description: One of the largest and most valuable tuna species. Overfishing has severely impacted their population.
endangeredFish.habitat: Open ocean waters in the Atlantic and Mediterranean
endangeredFish.distribution: Found in the Atlantic Ocean, Mediterranean Sea, and parts of the Pacific
endangeredFish.threats: Overfishing for sushi and sashimi markets, bycatch in fishing nets, climate change affecting spawning grounds
endangeredFish.importance: Top predator in marine ecosystems, maintains balance of fish populations. Culturally significant in Japan and Mediterranean regions.
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Fishing quotas and regulations, marine protected areas, aquaculture research, international cooperation agreements

endangeredFish.description: Historically abundant fish that has been overfished to near collapse in some areas. Slow to recover due to overfishing pressure.
endangeredFish.habitat: Cold waters of the North Atlantic Ocean
endangeredFish.distribution: Northwest Atlantic from Greenland to North Carolina, Northeast Atlantic around Europe
endangeredFish.threats: Overfishing, habitat destruction, climate change affecting water temperature
endangeredFish.importance: Key species in North Atlantic food webs, supports commercial fishing industries, important for ecosystem balance
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Fishing moratoriums, quota systems, marine reserves, gear restrictions to reduce bycatch

endangeredFish.description: Ancient species that was declared extinct in 2019. Lived in the Yangtze River for over 200 million years.
endangeredFish.distribution: Was endemic to the Yangtze River basin in China
endangeredFish.threats: Overfishing, dam construction blocking migration routes, pollution, habitat destruction
endangeredFish.importance: One of the largest freshwater fish species, important indicator of river ecosystem health, ancient evolutionary lineage
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Too late - species is now extinct. Serves as a warning about conservation efforts needed for other species

endangeredFish.description: Mysterious migratory fish that spawns in the Sargasso Sea. Population has declined dramatically due to multiple threats.
endangeredFish.habitat: Freshwater rivers and lakes in Europe, migrates to Sargasso Sea to spawn
endangeredFish.distribution: Europe, North Africa, and parts of Asia. Spawns in Sargasso Sea (Atlantic)
endangeredFish.threats: Habitat loss from dams and barriers, pollution, overfishing, climate change, parasites
endangeredFish.importance: Key part of freshwater and marine food chains, indicator species for water quality, culturally important in Europe
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Eel ladders and passages at barriers, fishing restrictions, habitat restoration, international eel management plans

endangeredFish.description: Large reef fish that can grow over 2 meters. Vulnerable due to slow growth and overfishing.
endangeredFish.habitat: Coral reefs and rocky areas in tropical and subtropical waters
endangeredFish.distribution: Indo-Pacific region, from Red Sea to Australia and Japan
endangeredFish.threats: Overfishing for live fish trade, habitat destruction of coral reefs, slow reproductive rate
endangeredFish.importance: Apex predator on coral reefs, maintains reef ecosystem balance, important for reef health
endangeredFish.conservationEfforts: Marine protected areas, size and catch limits, reef conservation programs, aquaculture alternatives
